text: Player: Back
Player: What did you see?
Player: BACK
Player: Default
Player: Back
We made our way back to the alleyway that Tymora brought us to a month ago.
The alley was quiet,[p/] but I wasn't sure if I could trust that.
We got looks from people living in the nearby buildings.[p/] They shut their windows the moment they saw us.[p/] I hoped I hadn't raised any alarms.
It would be too conspicuous to bring the Kingsguard with us.
Celeste actually planned the entire operation.[p/] They would walk through the streets and quickly storm the building the moment trouble arose.
They gave Celeste an enchanted Runestone to hold and channel her magic into.
If she ever stopped focusing her magic,[p/] that would send a signal to the Kingsguard to storm the building.
Celeste: Are you ready?
Tyrion: Yes...[p/] Let's do this...
The Eyetaker was our prime suspect.[p/] Everyone else had an alibi and lacked a motive.
Not to mention,[p/] Eris was with Beatrice when she went missing.
I didn't want to suspect her,[p/] but her father [i]was[/i] a criminal,[p/] and she still hadn't turned up since the massacre.
Tyrion: Alright.[p/] On three.
Tyrion: One.
Celeste: Two.
Tyrion: Three!
Player: Debris
Tyrion: There's some sort of debris on the ground.
I picked up one of the pieces and rolled it around in my palm.[p/] It appeared to be some kind of gravel.
Player: Detect Debris
Celeste: There are traces of [b]transmutation[/b] on this debris.
Tyrion: That would imply that a spell specifically targeted or created whatever this came from...
Tyrion: I doubt someone intentionally created these bits of gravel.
Tyrion: It must have broken off from something else.
Player: Balcony
Tyrion: This balcony provides a perfect view of Lake Galakrond.
Tyrion: A lot of people probably come here when the Grand Cathedral is open to the public.
Player: Back
Player: What did you see?
Tyrion: We heard that you saw the prosecutor acting strangely here.
Tyrion: Could you tell us more about what you saw?[p/] Did it look like she was afflicted with Mageblight?
Theon: I wouldn't go [i]that[/i] far.[p/] But she was certainly acting strangely.
Tyrion: How did you come across her?
Theon: Well, I was on my way to the Prayer Room.[p/] I always recite prayers from the draconic texts around that time.
...[i]He[/i] was heading to the Prayer Room?
Theon: When I saw her,[p/] I first thought she was just enjoying the view of the lake.
Theon: I was about to greet her,[p/] but when I approached her,[p/] I saw that she was [i]pushing[/i] something off the edge of the balcony.
Tyrion: You must have seen her dumping Sibyl's body...
Theon: You're [i]probably[/i] correct...
Theon: I came to the same conclusion after they found that poor woman's body below us.
Theon: But it feels wrong somehow...
Tyrion: What is it?
Theon: ...I only saw it for a second.
Theon: Lady Sibyl was wearing much brighter colors this morning.
Theon: Whatever Lady Steelwind threw into the lake,[p/] it looked much darker.
Tyrion: What does [i]that[/i] mean?
Theon: I'm sorry.[p/] I can't really explain it.
Theon: Perhaps I was just seeing things.[p/] My eyes aren't as good as they used to be, after all.
Theon: It could have just been a trick of the light.
Player: The Prayer Room
Tyrion: Wait...[p/] If you saw Aria dumping Lady Sibyl's body,[p/] she must have [i]just[/i] murdered her.
Tyrion: That means you must have been heading to where [i]we[/i] were at that time.
Theon: Oh?[p/] Were you praying there with Lord Pierce?
Tyrion: ...Not exactly.
Theon: What?[p/] That's not what the guards told me.
Tyrion: The guards?[p/] Do you mean the ones standing watch outside the Prayer Room?
Theon: Yes.[p/] They told me that Pierce was praying with some acquaintances of his.
Theon: They said the same thing to Lady Steelwind as well.
Tyrion: Wait,[p/][s/] [i]Steelwind[/i] was there too?!
Theon: Yes...[p/] She appeared to be waiting for something.
Theon: I didn't want to disturb them,[p/] so I went back to my quarters to pray there.
The High Speaker didn't see her fighting Celeste.
She must have started her attack [i]after[/i] he left,[p/] but why would she bother waiting?
Tyrion: Either way,[p/] we can't exactly question those guards.
Celeste: Yeah...[p/] They're still comatose after the attack.
Player: Why didn't you hear anything?
Tyrion: ...Actually,[p/] the High Speaker's Quarters aren't that far away from the Prayer Room.
Tyrion: Didn't you hear the fight between Steelwind and my bodyguard?
Theon: Ah...[p/] It must have been because of the magical wards in my room.
Tyrion: Magical wards?[p/] Are they there for security reasons?
Theon: Not exactly.
Theon: I actually suffer from chronic insomnia.[p/] Even the smallest sound will wake me up at night.
Theon: Years ago,[p/] I had some mages enchant the walls so sound couldn't travel through them.
Theon: I can't hear anything from outside the room once I shut my door.
...That did sound [i]awfully[/i] convenient,[p/] but I doubted that he'd lie about something so easily verified.
Player: BACK
Player: Runestone
Theon: A Runestone?
Tyrion: It's my Attorney's Runestone.
Theon: Ah,[p/] I recall the Duke of Commerce telling me about this device.
Theon: Artificery is yet another gift granted to us by the Scaled Lord.
Tyrion: But they only draw power from ley lines.[p/] It has nothing to do with Rathallion.
Theon: ...All arcane magic is derived from the ancient dragons.
Theon: To say otherwise is sacrilege.
Player: Tyrion
Tyrion: So you [i]have[/i] heard of me, High Speaker.
Theon: ...I have. [h="He's not|what I was expecting..."/]
Tyrion: You shouldn't believe every story you hear from the aristocracy.
Theon: ...I-[p/][s/]I don't know what you mean.
Player: Aria|Archeus's Encounter
Theon: I can't believe Lady Steelwind would do something like this...
Theon: ...But I can't really doubt what I saw with my own eyes.
Theon: Why was she acting so strangely?
Tyrion: ...There's more to this than we're all seeing.
Tyrion: And I'm going to get to the bottom of it.
Theon: For her sake,[p/] I hope you're right.
Player: Garrick|Autopsy Report: Garrick Pierce
Tyrion: Did you know Lord Pierce?[p/] He was one of the victims.
Theon: I didn't know him well.
Theon: Not to insult House Pierce,[p/] but he wasn't a noble of high standing.
Theon: I never had many opportunities to speak with him.
Player: Coraline|Autopsy Report: Coraline Sibyl
Tyrion: Did you know Coraline Sibyl?[p/] She was the head of one of the Four Pillar Families.
Theon: I did...[p/] It's quite unfortunate what happened to her.
Theon: Of the four families,[p/] I felt that House Sibyl was the one most loyal to the Church.
...For better or for worse.
Player: Lloyd|Autopsy Report: Lloyd von Sanctus
Tyrion: Did you know Lloyd von Sanctus? He was the head of House von Sanctus.
Theon: Yes. I knew him well.
Theon: We often worked together in matters of enterprise.
Tyrion: ...Why would the [i]Church[/i] need to discuss matters of enterprise?
Theon: ...Perhaps I've already said too much.
Player: Marriage Meeting
Theon: I [i]do[/i] hope the allegations against Ms. Steelwind are false.
Theon: She and His Majesty looked so happy this morning.
Celeste: ...Right.[p/] The King of Wyverngarde can't exactly marry a serial murderer.
She was the most resilient person I knew,[p/] but the possibility of losing her engagement with Aster probably weighed heavily in her mind.
Player: Debris Near the Balcony
Tyrion: Do you know where this debris came from?
Theon: I have no idea.
Theon: The groundskeepers are very diligent in keeping the Grand Cathedral clean. [i]Especially[/i], today.
Tyrion: Which means this debris must have fallen here recently...
Player: Soundproof Room
Tyrion: You must have been really desperate if you needed to soundproof your room.
Tyrion: How light of a sleeper are you?
Theon: So much so that the sounds of morning cicadas can wake me up at times.
Player: Theon
Tyrion: Forgive me if I'm being rude,[p/] but what [i]is[/i] a High Speaker?
Theon: You seriously don't know?[p/] How ignorant are you?
Tyrion: ...Okay, [i]that's[/i] a little rude.
Theon: The Church of Bahamut seeks to honor and revere the Scaled Lord Rathallion.
Theon: We are all called to spread the word of his greatness throughout Wyverngarde.
Theon: The High Speaker leads the Church through this mission.
Player: Back
Player: Default
Tyrion: What do you think about this, High Speaker?
Theon: My apologies.[p/] But there's not much to say about that.
We arrived at the west balcony of the cathedral.
Supposedly, [i]this[/i] was where the murderer threw Sibyl's body into the lake.
Theon: Oh...[p/] Hello, there.
As I inspected the area,[p/] I caught the attention of an old man nearby.
From his garb,[p/] he appeared to be someone who worked at the cathedral.
Theon: I...[p/] don't believe we've met before.[p/] What are you doing here?
Tyrion: Sorry for the intrusion.[p/] I'm Tyrion Cuthbert,[p/] and this is my bodyguard.
Celeste: ...We're investigating the murders on behalf of Prosecutor Steelwind.
Theon: Tyrion Cuthbert...[p/] I [i]have[/i] heard of you. [h="He doesn't look [i]nearly[/i] as criminal|as I expected him to be..."/]
He probably hadn't heard anything good...
Theon: ...But forgive my rudeness,[p/] I am Theon Archeus:[p/] High Speaker of the Church of Bahamut.
Tyrion: So [i]you're[/i] the one who saw Steelwind here during the murders.
Tyrion: Do you mind if we ask you some questions?
Theon: Hm...
Theon: ...I suppose there would be no harm in that.
I found everything I could here.[p/] It was time to MOVE somewhere else.
Player: Window
Tyrion: It's a stained glass window.[p/] A small ray of sunlight is shining through it.
Player: Seats
Tyrion: There are rows upon rows of seats for worshippers to sit at.
Tyrion: At full capacity,[p/] this room could probably host thousands of people at a time.
Player: Middle
Tyrion: There's an altar at the end of the room.
Tyrion: A religious figure would probably give a sermon here.
Player: Back
Player: What did Virgilae see?
Tyrion: Your Grace,[p/] where were you when the murders took place?
Alaric: Just what are you trying to insinuate?!
Remus: Please, Alaric.[p/] There's no need for that.
Remus: To answer your question,[p/] I was speaking with Lady Frega's familiar.
Tyrion: ...Tracker?
Remus: No...[p/] It was the demon who calls himself "Marrunath".
Tyrion: ...[i]What[/i]?!
Remus: He was actually the one who sent for me.
Remus: According to him,[p/] he had some information that was of great import to me.
Tyrion: ...Did you make a contract with him?
Remus: Of course not.[p/] He gave me the information he had freely...
Tyrion: ...Why?
Tyrion: Demons are supposedly always trying to trick humans into making deals with them.
Remus: Looking back,[p/] it must have been a distraction...
Remus: What a fool I was...[p/] I knew he was playing with me,[p/] but I knew he was unable to lie.
Remus: And the information he had was too important to ignore.
Tyrion: ...If you don't mind me asking,[p/] what was the information about?
Remus: ...
Remus: I'm sorry,[p/] but I won't reveal what he told me.
Remus: ...It would be a danger to everyone here.
Remus: But rest assured,[p/] it had nothing to do with the massacre.
Tyrion: Your Grace.[p/] We've only [i]just[/i] met.
Tyrion: I can't exactly just take your word for it.
Remus: I suppose that's fair,[p/] but believe me when I say that I plan to take this information to my grave.
...That was ominous,[p/] but he was clearly intent on keeping their conversation a secret.
Remus: Anyways,[p/] several members of the Kingsguard were watching us.
Remus: They can verify my alibi.
Tyrion: How long did this conversation last?
Remus: I arrived to speak with him at 1:50 PM,[p/] and I didn't leave until 3:10 PM.
So he was accounted for during the time frame of the murders...
But why would Marrunath go to the trouble of distracting him in the first place?
Whoever orchestrated this also used a [b]Force Cage[/b] to trap Aster...
Were they trying to distract the forces in the Cathedral?
Or were they trying to keep certain people out of the crossfire?
Player: Steelwind's spell
Tyrion: Your Highness.[p/] We heard the prosecutor cast a spell on you when you saw the fire in the Common Room.
Tyrion: Can you tell us more about what happened?
Alaric: I can't believe she [i]attacked[/i] a member of the royal family!
Alaric: She was completely irresponsible with her magic.
Tyrion: I understand your feelings about this,[p/] but we can't say for certain that she inflicted Mageblight on herself.
Tyrion: We don't even know if it was [i]actually[/i] her.
Alaric: I [i]saw[/i] her with my own eyes!
Alaric: Plenty of other people did too!
Tyrion: ...Either way,[p/] where did this happen?
Alaric: It was on the path outside the Common Room.
Alaric: I saw smoke coming from the building,[p/] and I saw Steelwind walking away from it.
So this must have been [i]after[/i] she murdered von Sanctus.
Tyrion: I have a feeling I already know the answer...[p/] But was she behaving erratically?
Alaric: No![p/][s/] She very [i]deliberately[/i] cast a spell on me!
Which once [i]again[/i] implies that Mageblight wasn't behind this...
Tyrion: Do you know which spell she cast on you?
Alaric: I'm not sure...
Alaric: I've never been affected by a spell like that before.
Remus: From the magical traces on him,[p/] we know that it was a [b]transmutation[/b] spell.
Celeste: [b]Transmutation[/b] spells manipulate and change physical matter...
Celeste: It's scary to think about what that might do to a human body.
Celeste: ...Are you sure he's okay?
Remus: Lord Asclepius inspected him,[p/] and he didn't appear to have any lasting injuries.
Remus: We're hoping the spell's effects were only temporary.
Tyrion: Can you describe what you felt while you were affected by it?
Tyrion: That might help us identify which spell it was.
Alaric: Well...[p/] I was extremely dazed,[p/] and my ears were ringing.
Alaric: My vision was also partially blurred.[p/] It was as if my mind was in some sort of fog.
Alaric: Whenever I tried to speak,[p/] I couldn't even form words with my mouth.
Alaric: I could only make grunting sounds.
Alaric: I couldn't walk either.[p/] So I had to crawl on the floor.
Tyrion: ...I'm sorry you had to go through that.[p/] That must have been traumatic.
Alaric: ...
But what kind of [b]transmutation[/b] spell would do that to him?
It had to be something that affected his senses.
Player: Steelwind's Demeanor
Tyrion: You already told us that she wasn't acting erratically,[p/] but was there anything off about her demeanor?
Alaric: I only saw her for a moment...
Alaric: But she was definitely different than usual.[p/] She was completely emotionless.
Celeste: ...Y-[p/]Yeah.[p/] That's exactly what she was like when I fought her, too.
Alaric: She was like an Arcane Construct.[p/] Her movements were almost robotic.
Tyrion: Wait...[p/] [i]Are[/i] there any Arcane Constructs on the Cathedral grounds?
Remus: No,[p/] not for lack of [i]trying[/i] on Lloyd's part.
Remus: The High Speaker and I have specifically forbidden the staff here from using them.
Hm...[p/] It would've been [i]really[/i] convenient if all of this was done using a Construct.
Tyrion: Anyways,[p/] was anyone else around when Steelwind cast that [b]transmutation[/b] spell on you?
Alaric: No.[p/] She moved away quickly after she incapacitated me.
Alaric: And even if someone [i]was[/i] around,[p/] I don't think I could've seen them anyways.
Commander White also said that he was surprised no one saw the prince until after that spell wore off.
What happened to him?
Player: Back
Player: Runestone
Alaric: ...Why are you showing this Runestone to me, knave?
Tyrion: It's my Attorney's Runestone.[p/] It proves that I'm a certified attorney.
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] Just like a peasant to fawn over such a monday-ed piece of artificery. [h="I want one..." e="s"/]
Player: Tyrion
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] Every time I see you,[p/] something horrible ends up happening.
Alaric: It's as if you bring trouble wherever you go.
Tyrion: My occupation just puts me in these positions.
Alaric: So you go around [i]looking[/i] for trouble then.
Celeste: ...Well,[p/] he's not wrong.
Player: Aria|Lloyd|Coraline|Garrick|Autopsy Report: Lloyd von Sanctus|Autopsy Report: Coraline Sibyl|Autopsy Report: Garrick Pierce
Alaric: I can't believe Lady Steelwind would do something like this...
Alaric: After everything my brother has done for her,[p/] [i]this[/i] is how she repays him?
Tyrion: There's more to this than we're all seeing.[p/] She can't be responsible for this.
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] We'll see.
Player: Aster
Alaric: Out of everyone,[p/] I think my brother has gotten the worst out of this.
Tyrion: Right...[p/] He has to choose between protecting his fiancée and maintaining an image of objectivity.
Remus: ...
Celeste: ...I hope he's okay.
Player: Remus
Tyrion: I can't believe I've gone this long without knowing who you are.
Tyrion: Why haven't I ever heard of you before, Your Grace?
Alaric: His Grace does not see it fit to mingle with the commonfolk.
Alaric: He only concerns himself with the matters [i]befitting[/i] of his station.
Remus: ...
Player: The Report|Alaric's Testimony|Alaric
Tyrion: It's a good thing you came out of this unharmed...
Tyrion: A lot of people lost their lives today.
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] Steelwind knows better than to harm a member of the royal family.
Back
Player: Default
Tyrion: What can you tell me about this, Your Highness?
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] Pondering such things is beneath someone of my stature.
He could have just said he has nothing to say...
Alaric: She attacked a member of the royal family and [i]killed[/i] several nobles!
Alaric: What [i]else[/i] is there to investigate?!
Alaric: The laws are clear about what we must do![p/][s/] We should execute her immediately!
Remus: Alaric.[p/] This situation is of far too much import to make such rash decisions.
Remus: We need to approach this calmly and carefully.
As we returned to the nave,[p/] I spotted the young prince stomping around as he argued with the nobleman I saw earlier.
Now that I thought about it,[p/] he was the only person here I didn't recognize...
Alaric: ...Oh, it's [i]you[/i] two.
Tyrion: Your Highness...
Tyrion: ...And I don't think [i]we've[/i] been introduced.
Remus: ...
Celeste: ...
Alaric: I see you're still as insolvent as ever...
Alaric: You stand before Sir Remus Virgilae:[p/] Archduke and Royal Visor to the Wyverngarde Throne.
Remus: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] I believe he means [i]ad[/i]visor.
"Archduke"?
I didn't even know the Kingdom of Wyverngarde [i]had[/i] a title like that.
Why hadn't I ever heard of him before?
Remus: ...Why [i]are[/i] you two still here anyways?
Remus: I thought the cathedral was closed for the investigation.
Tyrion: We're investigating what happened on behalf of Prosecutor Steelwind.
Remus: I...[p/] see.
Remus: Anyways,[p/] I have much to attend to.[p/] I'll be taking my leave.
Tyrion: ...Actually,[p/] since we have you here,[p/] we'd like to ask you some questions.
Tyrion: ...[i]Both[/i] of you.
Alaric: Hmph...[p/] You should know your place, knave.
Remus: ...
Tyrion: That covers [i]Alaric's[/i] account of what happened,[p/] and we even got the Archduke's statement too.
Tyrion: But there's still one last person we need to speak to.
Celeste: Aria...
Tyrion: She and Beatrice supposedly have no memories of what happened.
Tyrion: If it were only Beatrice saying that,[p/] I'd probably doubt it.
Tyrion: But if [i]Steelwind[/i] also doesn't have any memories,[p/] it becomes a lot more credible.
Tyrion: She definitely has the missing piece we need to put this together.
Remus: Are you going to the dungeon to speak with her?
Tyrion: ...I am.
Remus: Wonderful.[p/] I'll be accompanying you.
Tyrion: What?
Remus: As the royal advisor,[p/] I have a vested interest in the investigation.
Remus: While I trust Lucio to leave no stone unturned,[p/] I hear you have quite the talent for seeing what the Crown cannot.
Tyrion: ...
Player: Lucio Steelwind
Tyrion: ...
Lucio: ...Why are you looking at me like that, Cuthbert?
After he attacked me in Frega Manor,[p/] I'd always been a little wary of him.
If it weren't for Celeste's reflexes,[p/] he might have very well killed me that day...
Lucio: [i]*sigh*[/i][p/] I'm [i]not[/i] going to attack you again.
Celeste: ...
Tyrion: ...You [i]did[/i] almost kill me the last time we met.
Aster: What?
Lucio: That's not—!
Lucio: ...
Lucio: Something changed in you between Dracogen's trial and Frega's trial.[p/] Your aura became stronger.
Tyrion: ...What?
Player: Tyrion's Aura
Tyrion: What does my aura have to do with this?
Lucio: ...It's difficult to explain to someone like you,[p/] but warriors such as I must hone and develop our senses to survive in battle.
Lucio: Eventually,[p/] we develop the ability to sense hidden enemies and anticipate danger.
Lucio: I'd heard that such senses are heightened when in the presence of "extraplanar" entities.
Lucio: Until I met you and Beatrice Frega's familiar,[p/] I thought those stories were complete nonsense.
Tyrion: Marrunath and me?
Lucio: As a heavensborn,[p/] your blood is said to originate from the heavens.
Lucio: I wouldn't go so far as to say that I [i]believe[/i] in the existence of other planes.
Lucio: But I admit that I [i]still[/i] feel a certain aura from you that doesn't feel quite human.
Tyrion: Okay,[p/] but you've met me before in Aurinax.
Lucio: And something clearly changed since then.[p/] Even now,[p/] your aura is very noticeable.
...Wait.[p/] Was this because of that riddle I solved?
Did I gain the [b]Divine Edict[/b] because my powers had gotten stronger?
Lucio: Wyverngarde is no friend of the Theocracy.[p/] When I sensed you,[p/] I thought a cleric had somehow made it into the manor.
Lucio: Do you not sense it too, McCoy?
Celeste: ...What?
Lucio: Surely you noticed a change in him during the Lords' Ball.
Tyrion: ...Is that true?
Celeste: I [i]did[/i],[p/] but I didn't think it was a [i]magic[/i] thing.
Tyrion: What did you think it was?
Celeste: ...Well,[p/] sometimes you have someone in your life,[p/] and well—
Celeste: You start looking at them differently.
Tyrion: O-[p/][s/]Oh...
Lucio: ...
Lucio: I regret bringing this up.
Player: Aster as a suspect
Tyrion: Aster.[p/] I'm sure you know why I'm here.
Aster: It's because I'm one of your suspects, isn't it?!
Lucio: ...Your Majesty.[p/] You are the [i]reigning[/i] king of Wyverngarde.
Lucio: I expect you to conduct yourself with more dignity...
Aster: O-[p/][s/]Of course.[p/] My apologies, Grand General.
Tyrion: Anyways,[p/] questioning you is honestly a formality at this point.
Aster: ...Really?
Tyrion: A lot of the evidence works in your favor.
He didn't have a Blood Contract with the Empress of Discord after all.
Aster: Well, I appreciate your vote of confidence nonetheless.
Player: The Force Cage
Tyrion: Lucio,[p/] have you made any headway in figuring out who set that trap for Aster?
Lucio: No,[p/] I haven't.
Aster: ...It makes me uneasy that you let someone get [i]that[/i] close to my father's body.
Lucio: ...I can't begin to apologize for my men's incompetence.
Lucio: If they were more vigilant,[p/] perhaps we could have avoided this whole tragedy entirely.
Tyrion: So your men [i]were[/i] guarding the late king's coffin.
Lucio: Yes.[p/] There wasn't a single moment when his body was left unguarded.
Aster: We found traces of [b]illusion[/b] magic at the location of the [b]Force Cage[/b].
Aster: Although we're still not sure about [i]who[/i] could have set that trap.
Aster: ...There are also members of the Kingsguard that can cast both [b]Force Cage[/b] and [b]Glyph of Contingency[/b].
Aster: Many of them fell comatose during the massacre.
Lucio: Do you think my men are [i]that[/i] weak, Your Majesty?
Aster: ...I think anyone can be made weak when pushed far enough.
Lucio: Hmph...[p/] Regardless,[p/] we have no way of telling [i]who[/i] may have been guarding the coffin when the trap was set.
Lucio: This means that we have no way of telling [i]when[/i] that [b]Glyph of Contingency[/b] was set.
Tyrion: I was actually meaning to ask about that.[p/] Couldn't we wait for the forty-eight hours to elapse?
Tyrion: If we make note of the exact time the traces of [b]evocation[/b] disappear,[p/] we can work backwards to discern the exact time the trap was set.
Lucio: Well...[p/] [i]Normally[/i] we could.
Aster: When the spell activated,[p/] I unfortunately panicked and cast a [b]Fire Bolt[/b] to try to destroy it.
Aster: ...Of course,[p/] it didn't even put a dent in the [b]Force Cage[/b].
Lucio: Because of that,[p/] he left traces of [b]evocation[/b] at the exact location of the [b]Glyph of Contingency[/b].
Lucio: We can't discern which traces were left by the Patron's [b]Glyph of Contingency[/b] or His Majesty's [b]Fire Bolt[/b].
Aster: I'm terribly sorry about that.
I couldn't blame him for reacting like that,[p/] but it was going to make figuring out who did this that much more difficult.
Player: Aria Steelwind
Tyrion: ...I heard you two visited Aria earlier today.
Tyrion: How is she doing?
Tyrion: Have either of you spoken to Aria since the trial?
Aster: ...We have.
Aster: I think she's doing the best she can.[p/] She's quite the resilient girl.
Lucio: ...No thanks to you.
Aster: ...What?
Lucio: She's only [i]in[/i] this situation because of you.
Tyrion: Lucio...[p/] That's not—
Aster: ...Me?[p/][s/] You're the one who's heartlessly prosecuting her.

In the kingdom of Wyverngarde,[p/] there was a gulf between what was lawful and what was just.
People suffered.[p/] People died.[p/] All on behalf of the corrupt powers that ruled over us.
The lord of Kornhaven was drunk on the power of his station.[p/] He killed his friend the moment that power was threatened.
The headmaster of the Academy was cruelly thrown away—[p/]forced to realize that he built his legacy on borrowed authority.[p/] He committed unforgivable acts to cling to it.
To the Duke of Commerce,[p/] his people were no different than the gold within his coffers.[p/] In his hunger,[p/] he nearly sacrificed hundreds to sit upon a hollow throne.
Frega's inheritor had ambitions of grandeur and had proven herself worthy.[p/] Yet she was stripped of her power all the same.[p/] It drove her to seek liberation in hell.
Even the evilest demons were not responsible for the rot within Wyverngarde.[p/] They only revealed what was always there.
People feared becoming prey,[p/] and that anxiety suffocated them.[p/] Reality had always demanded compromise.
Some learned to numb themselves.[p/] Some learned how to exploit the world's cruelty.[p/] Some felt desperate enough to sell their souls to the devil.
It was a rigged game,[p/] and sometimes virtues like "law" or "justice" felt so alien that they might as well have been fiction.
